title: AWS Certified Solutions Architect - Associate
description: Scenario practice batch for Design Cost-Optimized Architectures.

questions.question: A development environment is used only during business hours on weekdays. Which change usually reduces cost the most with little risk?
answers: Move it to a larger instance family
Schedule the environment to stop when not in use
questions.shortExplanation: Stopping dev and test systems outside their active window is often the fastest and safest way to reduce waste.
questions.fullExplanation: Stopping dev and test systems outside their active window is often the fastest and safest way to reduce waste.
questions.explanation: Stopping dev and test systems outside their active window is often the fastest and safest way to reduce waste.

questions.question: A workload stores logs that are rarely read after 90 days but must remain accessible for audits. Which S3 feature should be configured?
questions.shortExplanation: Lifecycle transitions automatically move aging data to cheaper storage classes aligned to access frequency.
questions.fullExplanation: Lifecycle transitions automatically move aging data to cheaper storage classes aligned to access frequency.
questions.explanation: Lifecycle transitions automatically move aging data to cheaper storage classes aligned to access frequency.

questions.question: A batch analytics job is fault tolerant and can tolerate interruptions. Which EC2 pricing model is most cost effective?
questions.shortExplanation: Spot Instances are built for interruptible workloads and often provide the largest cost savings.
questions.fullExplanation: Spot Instances are built for interruptible workloads and often provide the largest cost savings.
questions.explanation: Spot Instances are built for interruptible workloads and often provide the largest cost savings.

questions.question: A company has steady baseline EC2 usage year-round. Which purchasing option should be evaluated to lower cost?
answers: Public IPv4 addresses for all instances
questions.shortExplanation: Commitment-based pricing works best against steady baseline usage where utilization is predictable.
questions.fullExplanation: Commitment-based pricing works best against steady baseline usage where utilization is predictable.
questions.explanation: Commitment-based pricing works best against steady baseline usage where utilization is predictable.

questions.question: A team wants to optimize cost but does not yet know which services are driving spend. What should they review first?
questions.shortExplanation: Cost Explorer and tagging provide the evidence needed to prioritize optimization work effectively.
questions.fullExplanation: Cost Explorer and tagging provide the evidence needed to prioritize optimization work effectively.
questions.explanation: Cost Explorer and tagging provide the evidence needed to prioritize optimization work effectively.

questions.question: A spiky API runs continuously on underutilized EC2 instances. Which redesign often improves cost efficiency?
answers: Migrate to Lambda or a more elastic managed service
questions.shortExplanation: Bursty workloads often benefit from pay-per-use compute so the business is not paying for idle baseline servers.
questions.fullExplanation: Bursty workloads often benefit from pay-per-use compute so the business is not paying for idle baseline servers.
questions.explanation: Bursty workloads often benefit from pay-per-use compute so the business is not paying for idle baseline servers.

questions.question: A storage team keeps every snapshot forever and costs keep rising. What control should be introduced?
answers: Snapshot retention policy and cleanup automation
questions.shortExplanation: Retention controls stop forgotten snapshots from accumulating silently over time.
questions.fullExplanation: Retention controls stop forgotten snapshots from accumulating silently over time.
questions.explanation: Retention controls stop forgotten snapshots from accumulating silently over time.

questions.question: A workload can run on Graviton without code changes. Why should the architect evaluate it?
answers: It removes the need for IAM
questions.shortExplanation: Graviton often delivers favorable price-performance and is a common cost optimization path.
questions.fullExplanation: Graviton often delivers favorable price-performance and is a common cost optimization path.
questions.explanation: Graviton often delivers favorable price-performance and is a common cost optimization path.

questions.question: An application sends large amounts of data across Regions unnecessarily. What is the likely result?
questions.shortExplanation: Cross-Region data transfer can materially increase total spend and should be minimized where possible.
questions.fullExplanation: Cross-Region data transfer can materially increase total spend and should be minimized where possible.
questions.explanation: Cross-Region data transfer can materially increase total spend and should be minimized where possible.

questions.question: A workload scales out correctly during peaks but never scales back in. What is the cost consequence?
questions.shortExplanation: Scale-out without scale-in defeats elasticity and leaves idle compute running after demand subsides.
questions.fullExplanation: Scale-out without scale-in defeats elasticity and leaves idle compute running after demand subsides.
questions.explanation: Scale-out without scale-in defeats elasticity and leaves idle compute running after demand subsides.

questions.question: A company uses warm standby in multiple Regions but business stakeholders only require recovery in 24 hours. What architecture concern should be revisited?
answers: Whether the resilience pattern is overbuilt for the RTO
questions.shortExplanation: If business recovery requirements are loose, an expensive warm-standby design may be more than the business actually needs.
questions.fullExplanation: If business recovery requirements are loose, an expensive warm-standby design may be more than the business actually needs.
questions.explanation: If business recovery requirements are loose, an expensive warm-standby design may be more than the business actually needs.

questions.question: A team plans to buy Savings Plans for an unpredictable workload that spikes heavily during one month each quarter. What is the key risk?
answers: They may commit above the steady baseline and underuse the commitment
The VPC endpoints will be deleted
questions.shortExplanation: Savings commitments should map to the predictable baseline, not temporary peaks, or the value erodes.
questions.fullExplanation: Savings commitments should map to the predictable baseline, not temporary peaks, or the value erodes.
questions.explanation: Savings commitments should map to the predictable baseline, not temporary peaks, or the value erodes.

questions.question: A company archives data in a very cheap tier but suddenly needs frequent retrieval. What tradeoff did the original design underestimate?
questions.shortExplanation: Low storage price is only one side of the equation; retrieval fees and restore times also matter.
questions.fullExplanation: Low storage price is only one side of the equation; retrieval fees and restore times also matter.
questions.explanation: Low storage price is only one side of the equation; retrieval fees and restore times also matter.

questions.question: A workload runs on self-managed databases even though the team spends many hours each week patching and maintaining them. Which cost concept is most relevant?
answers: Operational overhead should be included in cost decisions
questions.shortExplanation: True cost includes team time and operational risk, not just resource hourly rates.
questions.fullExplanation: True cost includes team time and operational risk, not just resource hourly rates.
questions.explanation: True cost includes team time and operational risk, not just resource hourly rates.

questions.question: A report shows many lightly used instances running around the clock. What should the architect examine first?
questions.shortExplanation: Low utilization is usually a sign that rightsizing or scheduled shutdown could immediately reduce cost.
questions.fullExplanation: Low utilization is usually a sign that rightsizing or scheduled shutdown could immediately reduce cost.
questions.explanation: Low utilization is usually a sign that rightsizing or scheduled shutdown could immediately reduce cost.
